_publications._publicationTitle: Interpreting mammalian target of rapamycin and cell growth inhibition in a genetically engineered mouse model of Nf1-deficient astrocytes.

_contextSnippet: we observed a significant decrease in the number of Ki67-immunoreactive (proliferating) cells ( Fig. 3A ) at 2mg/kg/day rapamycin. As shown above, 2mg/kg/day rapamycin did not inhibit mTOR signaling
details: Significant decrease in Ki67-immunoreactive cells observed at 2mg/kg/day rapamycin, despite this dose not inhibiting mTOR signaling, suggesting Ki67 may not be ideal marker for glial cell proliferation in NF1 studies
